stimulus: Geologists once believed that all volcanoes formed in the same way, but recent research suggests that hotspot volcanoes form quite differently from subduction-zone volcanoes. Subduction-zone volcanoes erupt explosively and produce thick, slow-moving lava. ______blank hotspot volcanoes erupt gently and produce thin, fast-flowing lava.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "In comparison" logically signals that the description of hotspot volcanoes in this sentence—that they erupt gently and produce thin lava—forms a comparison with the description of subduction-zone volcanoes in the previous sentence.

Choice B is incorrect because "for instance" illogically signals that hotspot volcanoes exemplify subduction-zone volcanoes; instead, they form a contrast. Choice C is incorrect because "firstly" illogically signals a first item in a series. Choice D is incorrect because "in conclusion" illogically signals a summary, when the sentence introduces a new comparison.
